quality.rationale: 利用光子波矢与相位沿零测地线不变性，结合观测者四维速度内积进行多步推导

origin.question:
[文件 1: 1_s8_8.28.md]

Problem 8.28. The redshift between two observers (with 4-velocities $\mathbf{u}_{\mathbf{A}}$ and $\mathbf{u}_{\mathbf{B}}$ ) can be defined in two ways: (i) by the energies of a photon (4-momentum $\mathbf{p}$ ) travelling along a null geodesic between them: $1 + z \equiv \mathbf{u}_{\mathbf{A}} \cdot \mathbf{p} / \mathbf{u}_{\mathbf{B}} \cdot \mathbf{p}$ , or (ii) by the proper time between two null geodesics, emitted $\Delta 	au_{\mathbf{A}}$ apart and received $\Delta 	au_{\mathbf{B}}$ apart, $1 + z \equiv \Delta 	au_{\mathbf{A}} / \Delta 	au_{\mathbf{B}}$ . Show that these definitions are equivalent.

## Solution
origin.solution:
Solution 8.28. Start with $1 + z = \Delta 	au_{\mathbf{A}} / \Delta 	au_{\mathbf{B}}$ . To show that this is equivalent to the other definition, recall that $\mathbf{p}$ is proportional to $\mathbf{k}$ , the wave vector, which is the gradient of the surfaces of constant phase:
$$
\mathbf {k} = \nabla \theta .
$$
Since $\mathbf{k}$ is a null vector,
$$
0 = \mathbf {k} \cdot \mathbf {k} = \mathbf {k} \cdot \nabla \theta = \nabla_{\mathbf {k}} \theta
$$
i.e. $	heta =$ constant along the rays. Consider two rays differing in phase by $\Delta 	heta$ . Observer A computes $\Delta 	heta$ as
$$
\Delta \theta = \theta (\tau + \Delta \tau_{\mathbf {A}}) - \theta (\tau) = (\Delta \tau_{\mathbf {A}}) \nabla_{\mathbf {u}_{\mathbf {A}}} \theta = (\Delta \tau_{\mathbf {A}}) \mathbf {u}_{\mathbf {A}} \cdot \mathbf {k}.
$$
![](images/19df07a68aa581e736e84e46b0dc2a8c946215ea8a02c952d4dd69a14863f30a.jpg)
Similarly observer B finds
$$
\Delta \theta = (\Delta \tau_{\mathbf {B}}) \mathbf {u}_{\mathbf {B}} \cdot \mathbf {k}
$$
where $\mathbf{k}$ is now evaluated at B. Since $\Delta 	heta$ is the same for A and B, we have
$$
\frac {\Delta \tau_{\mathbf {A}}}{\Delta \tau_{\mathbf {B}}} = \frac {(\mathbf {u} \cdot \mathbf {k})_{\mathbf {B}}}{(\mathbf {u} \cdot \mathbf {k})_{\mathbf {A}}} = \frac {(\mathbf {u} \cdot \mathbf {p})_{\mathbf {B}}}{(\mathbf {u} \cdot \mathbf {p})_{\mathbf {A}}}
$$
so the two definitions are equivalent.
